question: Find the NYC elementary school in District 15 that ranked 2nd by average performance category score over school years 2010-13, then find the park bounding the neighborhood associated with that school's ZIP code. Find the adjacent museum to that park, then compute how many years after the park opened that museum formally opened. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2010-2011-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
import re

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s

perf_col = [c for c in df.columns if "PERFORMANCE CATEGORY SCORE" in c.upper()][0]
level_col = [c for c in df.columns if "SCHOOL LEVEL" in c.upper()][0]
school_col = "SCHOOL" if "SCHOOL" in df.columns else "School Name"

filtered = df.copy()
filtered["DISTRICT"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["DISTRICT"], errors="coerce")
filtered[perf_col] = pd.to_numeric(filtered[perf_col], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered[
    filtered["DISTRICT"].eq(15)
    & filtered[level_col].astype(str).str.contains("Elementary", case=False, na=False)
]
filtered["_ps_num"] = filtered[school_col].astype(str).str.extract(r"P\.?S\.?\s*0*(\d+)", expand=False)
filtered["_ps_num"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["_ps_num"], errors="coerce").fillna(-1)
filtered = filtered.sort_values([perf_col, "_ps_num"], ascending=[False, False])
limit = 5
top = filtered[[school_col, perf_col]].dropna(subset=[school_col, perf_col]).head(limit).copy()
top[school_col] = top[school_col].astype(str).map(normalize_school)
answer = {row[school_col]: float(row[perf_col]) for _, row in top.iterrows()}
1.subquestion: Which NYC elementary schools in District 15 and their performance category scores in 2010-11?

import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2012-2013-citywide-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"

else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
import re

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s

perf_col = [c for c in df.columns if "PERFORMANCE CATEGORY SCORE" in c.upper()][0]
level_col = [c for c in df.columns if "SCHOOL LEVEL" in c.upper()][0]
school_col = "SCHOOL" if "SCHOOL" in df.columns else "School Name"

filtered = df.copy()
filtered["DISTRICT"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["DISTRICT"], errors="coerce")
filtered[perf_col] = pd.to_numeric(filtered[perf_col], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered[
    filtered["DISTRICT"].eq(15)

]
filtered["_ps_num"] = filtered[school_col].astype(str).str.extract(r"P\.?S\.?\s*0*(\d+)", expand=False)
filtered["_ps_num"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["_ps_num"], errors="coerce").fillna(-1)

4.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2013-2014-school-locations/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)

import re

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s.lower()

year_sources = [
    "datagov/2010-2011-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt",
    "datagov/2011-2012-school-progress-report-all-schools/files/rows.txt",
    "datagov/2012-2013-citywide-progress-report/files/rows.txt",
]

per_year = []
for ys in year_sources:
    y_local = ys if os.path.exists(ys) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + ys
    if os.path.exists(y_local):
        ydf = pd.read_csv(y_local, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
    else:
        yobj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=ys)
        ydf = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(yobj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)

    perf_col = [c for c in ydf.columns if "PERFORMANCE CATEGORY SCORE" in c.upper()][0]
    level_col = [c for c in ydf.columns if "SCHOOL LEVEL" in c.upper()][0]
    school_col = "SCHOOL" if "SCHOOL" in ydf.columns else "School Name"

    ydf["DISTRICT"] = pd.to_numeric(ydf["DISTRICT"], errors="coerce")
    ydf[perf_col] = pd.to_numeric(ydf[perf_col], errors="coerce")

    y = ydf[
        ydf["DISTRICT"].eq(15)
        & ydf[level_col].astype(str).str.contains("Elementary", case=False, na=False)
    ][[school_col, perf_col]].dropna(subset=[perf_col]).copy()
    y["school_norm"] = y[school_col].astype(str).map(normalize_school)
    y = y[["school_norm", perf_col]].rename(columns={perf_col: "score"})
    per_year.append(y)

# Keep schools with non-null scores in all three years.
common = set(per_year[0]["school_norm"]) & set(per_year[1]["school_norm"]) & set(per_year[2]["school_norm"])
frames = []
for i, y in enumerate(per_year):
    t = y[y["school_norm"].isin(common)].copy()
    t = t.rename(columns={"score": f"score_{i}"})
    frames.append(t)

merged = frames[0]
for t in frames[1:]:
    merged = merged.merge(t, on="school_norm", how="inner")

merged["avg_score"] = merged[["score_0", "score_1", "score_2"]].mean(axis=1)
merged = merged.sort_values("avg_score", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

target_norm = merged.iloc[1]["school_norm"]

df["_norm_loc"] = df["LOCATION_NAME"].astype(str).map(normalize_school)
row = df[df["_norm_loc"].eq(target_norm)].iloc[0]

loc_text = str(row.get("Location 1", ""))
match = re.search(r"\b(\d{5})(?:-\d{4})?\b", loc_text)
if match:
    answer = match.group(1)
else:
    addr_text = str(row.get("PRIMARY_ADDRESS_LINE_1", ""))
    match2 = re.search(r"\b(\d{5})(?:-\d{4})?\b", addr_text)
    answer = match2.group(1) if match2 else ""
4.subquestion: Among NYC elementary schools in District 15, which school ranks 2nd by average performance category score across 2010-13, and what is its zip code? Use <node_1 answer>, <node_2 answer>, <node_3 answer> as compact previews from previous hop.

5.fact:
Park Slope is a neighborhood in South Brooklyn, New York City, within the area once known as South Brooklyn. Park Slope is roughly bounded by Prospect Park and Prospect Park West to the east, Fourth Avenue to the west, Flatbush Avenue to the north, and Prospect Expressway to the south. Generally, the neighborhood is divided into three sections from north to south: North Slope, Center Slope, and South Slope.New York Visitors Guide, Park Slope & Prospect Heights , New York, Accessed June 23, 2009. "Boundaries, Park Slope: From 19th St. north to Flatbush Ave., From Prospect Park W. west to Fourth Ave"Park Slope neighborhood profile , New York, extracted from a March 10, 2003 article. Accessed September 25, 2007. "Boundaries: Stretching from Prospect Park West to Fourth Avenue, Park Place to Prospect Expressway."Oser, Alan N. "Rezoning, and Redefining, Park Slope", The New York Times, December 28, 2003. Accessed March 26, 2025. "As broadly defined by brokers marketing real estate there, Park Slope is bordered by Flatbush Avenue to the north, the Prospect Expressway to the south, Prospect Park and Prospect Park West to the east, and Fourth Avenue to the west. The April rezoning actually extends west as far as Third Avenue on some blocks, and only as far as 15th Street to the south." The neighborhood takes its name from its location on the western slope of neighboring Prospect Park. Fifth Avenue and Seventh Avenue are its primary commercial streets, while its east–west side streets are lined with brownstones and apartment buildings.

Park Slope is part of Brooklyn Community District 6, and its primary ZIP Codes are 11215 and 11217. It is patrolled by the 78th Precinct of the New York City Police Department. Politically, it is represented by the New York City Council's 33rd and 39th Districts.Current City Council Districts for Kings County , New York City. Accessed May 5, 2017.
5.subquestion: Which park gives its name to the neighborhood whose primary ZIP codes include <node_4 answer>?

6.fact:
Prospect Park is a  urban park in the New York City borough of Brooklyn. The park is situated between the neighborhoods of Park Slope, Prospect Heights, Crown Heights, Prospect Lefferts Gardens, Flatbush, and Windsor Terrace, and is adjacent to the Brooklyn Museum, Grand Army Plaza, and the Brooklyn Botanic Garden. With an area of , Prospect Park is the second-largest public park in Brooklyn, behind Marine Park. Designated as a New York City scenic landmark and listed on the National Register of Historic Places, Prospect Park is operated by the Prospect Park Alliance and NYC Parks.

First proposed in legislation passed in 1859, Prospect Park was laid out by landscape architects Frederick Law Olmsted and Calvert Vaux for the then-independent city of Brooklyn. Prospect Park opened in 1867, though it was not substantially complete until 1873. The park subsequently underwent numerous modifications and expansions to its facilities. Several additions to the park were completed in the 1890s, in the City Beautiful architectural movement. In the early 20th century, New York City Department of Parks and Recreation (NYC Parks) commissioner Robert Moses started a program to clean up Prospect Park. A period of decline in the late 20th century spurred the creation of the Prospect Park Alliance, which refurbished many parts of the park from the 1980s through the 2020s.
6.subquestion: Which museum is adjacent to <hop 3 answer>, and in what year did <hop 3 answer> open?

7.fact:
The Brooklyn Museum is an art museum in the New York City borough of Brooklyn. At , the museum is New York City's second largest and contains an art collection with around 500,000 objects. Located near the Prospect Heights, Crown Heights, Flatbush, and Park Slope neighborhoods of Brooklyn, the museum's Beaux-Arts building was designed by McKim, Mead & White.

The Brooklyn Institute museum's building was completed in March 1897 after a sidewalk was built between the museum's entrance and Eastern Parkway. The museum's first exhibit was a collection of almost 600 paintings, which had opened to the public on June 1, 1897, several months before the formal opening of the museum. The Brooklyn Institute's museum formally opened on October 2, 1897, and was one of the last major structures built in the city of Brooklyn before the formation of the City of Greater New York in 1898.
Computation: 1897 - 1867 = 30.
7.subquestion: How many years after the park opening year in <hop 4 answer> did the museum in <hop 4 answer> formally open?
